summary: Tested Pastor Creation Flow simplification from 9 steps to 3 steps. Fixed critical backend bug (KeyError: 'owner_id') in PUT/DELETE /api/pastors endpoints. All 13 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend flow verified: Step 1 (Basic Identity), Step 2 (Profile & Media), Step 3 (Review & Publish), Back button navigation, Save Draft, and Publish all working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: KeyError 'owner_id' in server.py update_pastor() and delete_pastor() - changed from pastor['owner_id'] to pastor.get('owner_id') to handle legacy data without owner_id field (same pattern used in church endpoints)
seed_data_creation: None - created test pastors prefixed with TEST_ and cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pastor Creation Flow at /listing/pastor/create works with 3 steps: 1) Basic Identity (name, email, phone required) - creates draft via POST /api/pastors, 2) Profile & Media (bio, photos, social, education, skills, experience, languages, roles) - updates via PUT /api/pastors/{id}, 3) Review & Publish - publishes with status=published. Save Draft button saves all form data. Published pastors appear in /api/pastors?status=published and /api/admin/pastors.

bug_fixed.issue: KeyError: 'owner_id' when accessing pastor['owner_id'] for pastors without owner_id field
bug_fixed.fix: Changed to pastor.get('owner_id') with conditional check, matching pattern in church endpoints
bug_fixed.root_cause: Legacy pastors or pastors created through different flows may not have owner_id field set
